title: Mastering Intraday Trading in India: Pro Tips for NSE & BSE
description: Learn the most effective intraday trading strategies for the Indian stock market. Discover how to identify high-momentum stocks and manage risk.
content:
Intraday trading requires extreme discipline, robust risk management, and the ability to read market momentum. Unlike swing trading, you have to square off your positions before the market closes at 3:30 PM.

1. Focus on High Liquidity Stocks

Always trade in Nifty 50 or high-beta stocks. High liquidity ensures that your orders are executed instantly without slippage. Illiquid stocks can trap you in upper or lower circuits.

2. The 1% Risk Rule

Never risk more than 1% of your total trading capital on a single trade. If your capital is INR 1,00,000, your maximum stop-loss hit for one trade should not exceed INR 1,000.

3. Use Strict Stop-Losses

The golden rule of intraday trading is to cut your losses short. Always use a system stop-loss. Mental stop-losses fail when emotions take over.

4. Leverage Premium Signals

If you don't have the time to analyze charts, relying on premium, verified signals (like those provided by Stockboy) can give you exact Entry, Stop-Loss, and Target levels.
author: Stockboy Research

title: Understanding F&O Signals: Options Trading Strategies
description: A comprehensive guide to Futures and Options (F&O) trading. Learn how to leverage Bank Nifty and Nifty 50 options safely.
content:
Futures and Options (F&O) offer massive leverage, allowing traders to generate significant ROI with smaller capital. However, option buying involves time decay (Theta), making timing crucial.

Why Bank Nifty?

Bank Nifty is the most volatile index in the Indian market. It respects technical levels beautifully but requires fast execution. This is why our premium members receive instant Telegram alerts.

Risk Management in Options

Option premiums can wipe out rapidly on expiry days (Wednesdays for Bank Nifty, Thursdays for Nifty 50). Only deploy capital you can afford to lose on hero-zero trades, and strictly follow the 1:3 risk-reward ratio.
author: Stockboy Research

title: Trading Psychology: Why 90% of Traders Lose Money
description: Discover the psychological pitfalls of stock trading. Learn how to control FOMO, revenge trading, and emotional decision making.
content:
The SEBI report stated that 9 out of 10 retail traders lose money in the F&O segment. The reason isn't always bad strategy—it's bad psychology.

FOMO (Fear of Missing Out)

Chasing green candles is a surefire way to lose capital. Let the trade come to your setup. If you miss a rally, accept it and wait for the next opportunity.

Revenge Trading

When a stop-loss is hit, the immediate urge is to take another trade to recover the loss. This usually leads to double losses. Accept the loss, close the terminal, and come back tomorrow.

The Stockboy Advantage

By following our premium signals, you remove the emotional burden of analysis. You execute the trade when the alert comes, set the targets, and let the system work.
author: Stockboy Research
